elements.sent-2411-001: We show that the second Jacobian ideal of a hypersurface can be decomposed such that a power of the Jacobian ideal becomes a factor.
elements.sent-2411-002: As an application of the decomposition, we present an elementary proof establishing that the second Nash blow-up algebra of a hypersurface singularity is a contact invariant.

elements.sent-2411-005: The $k$-th Nash blow-ups and their properties in various contexts have been investigated in recent literature, such as, \cite{Yasuda2007 , \cite{Barajas2020 , and \cite{Le2023 .}}}

elements.sent-2411-009: In this paper, we focus on the second Jacobian matrices of hypersurfaces and demonstrate that the second Jacobian ideal can be decomposed into the Jacobian ideal and an ideal involving second-order partial derivatives of $F$.
elements.sent-2411-010: The motivation of this project stems from a conjecture by N.. Hussain, G.

elements.sent-2411-517: By \cite[Corollary 2.20]{Le2024 , \begin{equation}J_2(G) = J_2(F\circ \phi).\end{equation}. Consequently, \begin{equation}O_n/(G, J_2(G)) = O_n/(F\circ\phi, J_2(F\circ \phi)).\end{equation}.}

elements.sent-2411-519: By Theorem \ref{thm:main , it follows that \begin{equation}J_2(F\circ\phi)=J_1(F\circ\phi)^{n+1} + J_1(F\circ\phi)^{n-2}Q(F\circ\phi)\subseteq \phi^*(J_1(F)^{n+1} + J_1(F)^{n-2}Q(F)) = \phi^*J_2(F).\end{equation}.}
